comment: Quality Gates catalog -- docs-repro dimension. Schema spec: quality/catalogs/README.md. Verifier impl: quality/gates/docs-repro/. Reports: quality/reports/verifications/docs-repro/.

asserts: in a fresh ubuntu:24.04 container with cargo + python3 + curl pre-installed and the workspace cargo build complete, bash examples/01-shell-loop/run.sh exits 0
the run leaves a partial-clone working tree at /tmp/reposix-example-01
the example pushes a commit and the simulator's audit log shows a helper_push_* row

rows.owner_hint: if RED: examples/01-shell-loop/run.sh has a regression OR the simulator/cli/helper changed and the example was not updated; check examples/01-shell-loop/expected-output.md and the binary on PATH preamble

waiver.reason: RENEWED P90 90-05 (2026-07-04): still-broken per R2 § D live re-check -- container-rehearse.sh never brings up the external simulator (reposix-sim listening on 127.0.0.1:7878) that the example script assumes; the P59-tracked 'Wave F CI rehearsal' work never landed and the 'v0.12.1' cliff label was dead. Locally the verifier exits non-zero with stderr signal 'sim not reachable'; in CI a sim service container would need to bring the dependency up, which it does not today.
waiver.tracked_in: P95 (Quality framework upgrade + doc fixes) -- RBF-D-14 RAISE LIST drain, quality/reports/raise-list-p90.md § 1

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the example actually worked end to end, bash examples/01-shell-loop/run.sh would exit 0 and the simulator's audit log would show a helper_push_* row; the observed 'sim not reachable' abort directly falsifies the 'exits 0' claim, which is why this row is WAIVED rather than PASS.

asserts: in a fresh ubuntu:24.04 container, python3 examples/02-python-agent/run.py exits 0
the agent finds 'database' in some issue body and applies a severity label
git push reports success

rows.owner_hint: if RED: examples/02-python-agent/run.py has a regression OR the simulator/cli/helper changed; check the python stdlib-only constraint and the binary on PATH preamble

waiver.reason: RENEWED P90 90-05 (2026-07-04): still-broken per R2 § D live re-check -- same root cause as example-01 (container-rehearse.sh never brings up the external simulator the example assumes); the 'v0.12.1'-tracked Wave F CI rehearsal work never landed.
waiver.tracked_in: P95 (Quality framework upgrade + doc fixes) -- RBF-D-14 RAISE LIST drain, quality/reports/raise-list-p90.md § 1

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the agent actually applied a severity label and pushed successfully, the run.py process would exit 0 and 'git push' would report success in its stdout; the same sim-not-reachable abort as example-01 falsifies both asserts directly, which is why this row is WAIVED rather than PASS.

asserts: examples/03-claude-code-skill/RUN.md exists and references .claude/skills/reposix-agent-flow
examples/03-claude-code-skill/agent-prompt.md exists and parses as markdown
this row is graded by manual verifier-subagent dispatch (not auto-runnable)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: examples/03-claude-code-skill/{RUN.md, agent-prompt.md} got out of sync with .claude/skills/reposix-agent-flow

asserts: bash examples/04-conflict-resolve/run.sh exits 0 in ubuntu:24.04 container
the second agent observes 'fetch first' from helper stderr and recovers via git pull --rebase
after recovery, both edits are merged

rows.owner_hint: if RED: push-time conflict detection regressed; check the helper export-path conflict logic

waiver.reason: RENEWED P90 90-05 (2026-07-04): still-broken per R2 § D live re-check -- same container-never-brings-up-sim root cause as example-01/02; the 'v0.12.1'-tracked Wave F CI rehearsal work never landed.
waiver.tracked_in: P95 (Quality framework upgrade + doc fixes) -- RBF-D-14 RAISE LIST drain, quality/reports/raise-list-p90.md § 1

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If conflict recovery actually worked, the second agent would observe 'fetch first' from helper stderr and both edits would be merged after 'git pull --rebase'; the container's sim-not-reachable abort means neither observable ever occurs, directly falsifying both asserts.

asserts: bash examples/05-blob-limit-recovery/run.sh exits 0 in ubuntu:24.04 container
the agent observes the blob-limit error from helper stderr and recovers via git sparse-checkout
after recovery, the targeted issue blob is materialized while siblings stay sparse

rows.owner_hint: if RED: blob-limit error message regressed OR sparse-checkout recovery path regressed

waiver.reason: RENEWED P90 90-05 (2026-07-04): still-broken per R2 § D live re-check -- same container-never-brings-up-sim root cause as example-01/02/04; the 'v0.12.1'-tracked Wave F CI rehearsal work never landed.
waiver.tracked_in: P95 (Quality framework upgrade + doc fixes) -- RBF-D-14 RAISE LIST drain, quality/reports/raise-list-p90.md § 1

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If blob-limit recovery actually worked, the agent would observe the blob-limit error and the targeted issue blob would materialize while siblings stay sparse after 'git sparse-checkout'; the container's sim-not-reachable abort means neither observable ever occurs, directly falsifying both asserts.

sources: (historical: scripts/repro-quickstart.sh -- migrated to this gate by SIMPLIFY-06; see commit history)

asserts: step 2: simulator starts and listens on 127.0.0.1:7878 (or alternate port)
step 3: reposix init sim::demo /tmp/repo exits 0 and configures partial-clone
step 4: git checkout -B main refs/reposix/origin/main exits 0
step 5: 0001.md exists with title: frontmatter
step 7: git push reports [new branch] main -> main
step 8: audit_events_cache has a row with op LIKE 'helper_push_%'

rows.owner_hint: if RED: docs/tutorials/first-run.md or one of the binary build steps regressed; check git history for scripts/repro-quickstart.sh (predecessor migrated by SIMPLIFY-06)

waiver.reason: RENEWED P90 90-05 (2026-07-04): still-broken, TWO causes per R2 § D live re-check -- (1) cold cargo build inside a fresh ubuntu:24.04 container still exceeds the 5-min container budget when not pre-warmed; (2) the push step (tutorial step 7) is additionally blocked by QL-001 (path-shape bug, routed to P91, D90-01). The 'v0.12.1'-tracked Wave F CI rehearsal work never landed.
waiver.tracked_in: P95 (Quality framework upgrade + doc fixes) -- RBF-D-14 RAISE LIST drain for the cold-build-budget cause; P91 QL-001 fix required before cause (2) clears; see quality/reports/raise-list-p90.md § 1

rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the tutorial actually replayed clean, steps 2-8 (sim up, init, checkout, file materialized, push success, audit row) would each observably succeed; a >5min cold-build timeout or a QL-001 push failure directly falsifies the corresponding step's assert, which is why this row is WAIVED rather than PASS.

asserts: docs/benchmarks/latency.md frontmatter last_measured_at is < 30 days old
the cached-read p50 cell value matches the headline 8 ms to within +/-2ms

rows.owner_hint: if RED: docs/benchmarks/latency.md drifted from headline numbers; v0.12.1 perf cross-check (perf/headline-numbers-cross-check) automates this row D-CONV-2 (2026-07-04): this row is the reason the weekly verdict is permanently yellow, not red-or-green -- intentionally NOT waived/softened (OD-4 SS3: mechanically verifying headline numbers is the launch-readiness milestone's job). Plan: GOOD-TO-HAVES-04 in .planning/milestones/v0.13.0-phases/GOOD-TO-HAVES.md routes mechanizing this row (real verifier.script, reusing perf/headline-numbers-cross-check.py's extraction logic) to the launch-readiness milestone.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the headline 8ms cached-read claim were false, either assert would falsify it: a stale last_measured_at (>=30d) means the reviewer can no longer trust the recorded number and must FAIL pending re-measurement, and a p50 cell value outside 8ms+/-2ms directly contradicts the docs/index.md and README.md headline text the row exists to police.

asserts: headline 89.1% is greppable from docs/benchmarks/token-economy.md
the comparison fixture file referenced from token-economy.md exists

rows.owner_hint: if RED: docs/benchmarks/token-economy.md drifted from headline numbers; v0.12.1 perf cross-check (perf/headline-numbers-cross-check) automates this row D-CONV-2 (2026-07-04): this row is the reason the weekly verdict is permanently yellow, not red-or-green -- intentionally NOT waived/softened (OD-4 SS3: mechanically verifying headline numbers is the launch-readiness milestone's job). Plan: GOOD-TO-HAVES-04 in .planning/milestones/v0.13.0-phases/GOOD-TO-HAVES.md routes mechanizing this row (real verifier.script, reusing perf/headline-numbers-cross-check.py's extraction logic) to the launch-readiness milestone.
rows.claim_vs_assertion_audit: If the 89.1% token-reduction headline were false, the reviewer's grep for the exact figure in docs/benchmarks/token-economy.md would come back empty (the number was never actually printed where the headline claims it lives), or the comparison fixture backing the calculation would be missing -- either finding directly falsifies the claim and flips the row to FAIL rather than PASS.

asserts: every fenced code block in user-facing docs has a catalog row in quality/catalogs/docs-reproducible.json or is matched by an allow-list entry
no catalogued snippet drifted from its source (sha256 match)

rows.owner_hint: if RED: a doc edit added a fenced code block without a catalog row OR a catalogued row's source content drifted; fix by either pasting the --write-template output or rolling back the doc edit. If the new block is illustrative, add the derived_id to quality/catalogs/docs-reproducible-allowlist.json with a reason.
